content: Phase IV trials and post-marketing surveillance occur after regulatory approval, monitoring drug safety and effectiveness in real-world clinical practice. These studies can be required by regulators (post-marketing requirements, PMRs) or initiated by manufacturers (post-marketing commitments, PMCs). Phase IV evaluates long-term safety, rare adverse events not detected in pre-approval trials, effectiveness in broader populations, optimal use patterns, and comparative effectiveness versus other treatments. Pharmacovigilance systems continuously collect and analyze adverse event reports from healthcare providers, patients, and literature.
